creator: itetcu@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Mon, 23 May 2011 01:25:20 +0200
Matthias Andree <mandree@FreeBSD.org> wrote:

> fresh 8-STABLE build, Skype sound works, but "Visit account page"
> yields this on standard output or error, and does not cause any other
> visible action (like starting a browser or such):
> 
> /libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Cannot open "/usr/lib/libv4l/v4l2convert.so"

What I get (under KDE 3.5.10) is:
/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Cannot open "/usr/lib/libv4l/v4l2convert.so"
/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Cannot open "/usr/lib/libv4l/v4l2convert.so"
/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Cannot open "/usr/lib/libv4l/v4l2convert.so"
/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Cannot open "/usr/lib/libv4l/v4l2convert.so"
/usr/local/bin/xdg-open: line 321: test: : integer expression expected
/usr/local/bin/xdg-open: line 322: test: : integer expression expected
/usr/local/bin/xdg-open: line 323: test: : integer expression expected

And yes, no browser opens, etc. but at least skype doesn't crash.

Do you use KDE? What version?

The xdg-open code is this:
>>>>
#----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# kfmclient exec/openURL can give bogus exit value in KDE <= 3.5.4
# It also always returns 1 in KDE 3.4 and earlier
# Simply return 0 in such case

kfmclient_fix_exit_code()
{
    version=`kde-config --version 2>/dev/null | grep KDE`
    major=`echo $version | sed 's/KDE: \([0-9]\).*/\1/'`
    minor=`echo $version | sed 's/KDE: [0-9]*\.\([0-9]\).*/\1/'`
    release=`echo $version | sed 's/KDE: [0-9]*\.[0-9]*\.\([0-9]\).*/\1/'`
    test "$major" -gt 3 && return $1
    test "$minor" -gt 5 && return $1
    test "$release" -gt 4 && return $1
    return 0
}
>>>>

I'll try to truss this, but I don't think there's much we can do about it.
And no, I have no idea why skype would try to access the video device
to open a browser :)

--
